exercises.title: Kraus Operator Implementation

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma: float) -> list:

    """

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma: float) -> list:
    """
    Return Kraus operators for amplitude damping.
    """
    E0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    E1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    return [E0, E1]

def phase_damping_kraus(lambda_p: float) -> list:
    """
    Return Kraus operators for phase damping.
    """
    E0 = np.sqrt(1-lambda_p) * np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
    E1 = np.sqrt(lambda_p) * np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    E2 = np.sqrt(lambda_p) * np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
    return [E0, E1, E2]

def depolarizing_kraus(p: float) -> list:
    """
    Return Kraus operators for depolarizing channel.
    """
    sigma_x = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
    sigma_y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]], dtype=complex)
    sigma_z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)
    I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
    
    E0 = np.sqrt(1 - 3*p/4) * I
    E1 = np.sqrt(p/4) * sigma_x
    E2 = np.sqrt(p/4) * sigma_y
    E3 = np.sqrt(p/4) * sigma_z
    return [E0, E1, E2, E3]

def verify_kraus_completeness(kraus_ops: list) -> bool:
    """
    Verify that sum_k E_k^dag E_k = I.
    """
    dim = kraus_ops[0].shape[0]
    total = sum(E.conj().T @ E for E in kraus_ops)
    return np.allclose(total, np.eye(dim))

def apply_channel(rho: np.ndarray, kraus_ops: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Apply quantum channel: rho -> sum_k E_k rho E_k^dag
    """
    result = np.zeros_like(rho)
    for E in kraus_ops:
        result += E @ rho @ E.conj().T
    return result

# Test implementations
print("Testing Kraus operators:")
for gamma in [0.0, 0.3, 0.7, 1.0]:
    kraus_ad = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma)
    kraus_pd = phase_damping_kraus(gamma)
    kraus_dp = depolarizing_kraus(gamma)
    
    print(f"\ngamma = {gamma}:")
    print(f"  Amp damp complete: {verify_kraus_completeness(kraus_ad)}")
    print(f"  Phase damp complete: {verify_kraus_completeness(kraus_pd)}")
    print(f"  Depolarizing complete: {verify_kraus_completeness(kraus_dp)}")

# Test channel effects
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("Channel effects on |1> state:")
rho1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)

for gamma in [0.3, 0.7]:
    print(f"\ngamma = {gamma}:")
    
    # Amplitude damping
    rho_ad = apply_channel(rho1, amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma))
    print(f"  Amp damp: P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_ad[1,1]):.4f}")
    
    # Phase damping
    rho_pd = apply_channel(rho1, phase_damping_kraus(gamma))
    print(f"  Phase damp: P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_pd[1,1]):.4f}")
    
    # Depolarizing
    rho_dp = apply_channel(rho1, depolarizing_kraus(gamma))
    print(f"  Depolarizing: P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_dp[1,1]):.4f}")

print("\n" + "="*50)
print("Channel effects on |+> state (coherence):")
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_plus = ket_plus @ ket_plus.conj().T

for gamma in [0.3, 0.7]:
    print(f"\ngamma = {gamma}:")
    
    for name, kraus_fn in [('Amp damp', amplitude_damping_kraus),
                            ('Phase damp', phase_damping_kraus),
                            ('Depolarizing', depolarizing_kraus)]:
        rho_out = apply_channel(rho_plus, kraus_fn(gamma))
        coh = np.abs(rho_out[0, 1])
        pur = np.real(np.trace(rho_out @ rho_out))
        print(f"  {name}: coherence = {coh:.4f}, purity = {pur:.4f}")
hints: Amplitude damping E0 = [[1, 0], [0, sqrt(1-gamma)]], E1 = [[0, sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]]
Depolarizing uses Pauli matrices with coefficients sqrt(p/4)
Apply channel: rho' = sum_k E_k rho E_k^dag

exercises.title: T1 and T2 Decay Simulation
exercises.description: Simulate qubit dynamics with T1 and T2 relaxation and verify the T2 <= 2*T1 constraint.
exercises.starterCode:

def t1_t2_evolution(rho0: np.ndarray, T1: float, T2: float, 
                    times: np.ndarray) -> list:
    """
    Evolve density matrix with T1 and T2 decay.
    Assumes zero-temperature bath (decay to |0>).
    
    Returns list of density matrices at each time.
    """

    pass

def extract_observables(rho_list: list) -> dict:
    """
    Extract P(|1>), coherence, and purity from list of density matrices.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def verify_t2_constraint(T1: float, T2: float) -> dict:
    """
    Check if T2 <= 2*T1 and calculate pure dephasing time.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Simulation
T1 = 100.0  # microseconds
T2_values = [30.0, 80.0, 150.0, 200.0]  # Test various T2

# Initial |+> state
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
rho0 = ket_plus @ ket_plus.conj().T

times = np.linspace(0, 300, 100)

for T2 in T2_values:
    constraint = verify_t2_constraint(T1, T2)
    print(f"\nT1={T1}, T2={T2}:")
    print(f"  Valid (T2 <= 2*T1): {constraint['valid']}")
    if constraint['valid']:
        print(f"  T_phi = {constraint['T_phi']:.2f}")
    
    rho_list = t1_t2_evolution(rho0, T1, T2, times)
    obs = extract_observables(rho_list)
    print(f"  Final P(|1>): {obs['p1'][-1]:.4f}")
    print(f"  Final coherence: {obs['coherence'][-1]:.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def t1_t2_evolution(rho0: np.ndarray, T1: float, T2: float, 
                    times: np.ndarray) -> list:
    """
    Evolve density matrix with T1 and T2 decay.
    """
    results = []
    
    # Initial values
    p1_0 = np.real(rho0[1, 1])
    rho01_0 = rho0[0, 1]
    
    for t in times:
        rho = np.zeros((2, 2), dtype=complex)
        
        # Populations (T1 decay to ground state)
        rho[1, 1] = p1_0 * np.exp(-t / T1)
        rho[0, 0] = 1 - rho[1, 1]
        
        # Coherences (T2 decay)
        rho[0, 1] = rho01_0 * np.exp(-t / T2)
        rho[1, 0] = np.conj(rho[0, 1])
        
        results.append(rho)
    
    return results

def extract_observables(rho_list: list) -> dict:
    """
    Extract observables from density matrix evolution.
    """
    p1 = [np.real(rho[1, 1]) for rho in rho_list]
    p0 = [np.real(rho[0, 0]) for rho in rho_list]
    coherence = [np.abs(rho[0, 1]) for rho in rho_list]
    purity = [np.real(np.trace(rho @ rho)) for rho in rho_list]
    
    return {
        'p1': np.array(p1),
        'p0': np.array(p0),
        'coherence': np.array(coherence),
        'purity': np.array(purity)
    }

def verify_t2_constraint(T1: float, T2: float) -> dict:
    """
    Check T2 <= 2*T1 and calculate pure dephasing time.
    """
    valid = T2 <= 2 * T1 + 1e-10
    
    if valid and T2 < 2 * T1:
        # T_phi from 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi
        T_phi = 1 / (1/T2 - 1/(2*T1))
    else:
        T_phi = np.inf  # No pure dephasing needed
    
    return {
        'valid': valid,
        'T_phi': T_phi,
        'T2_max': 2 * T1
    }

# Simulation
T1 = 100.0
T2_values = [30.0, 80.0, 150.0, 200.0]

ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
rho0 = ket_plus @ ket_plus.conj().T

times = np.linspace(0, 300, 100)

print("T1/T2 Relaxation Analysis")
print("="*60)

for T2 in T2_values:
    constraint = verify_t2_constraint(T1, T2)
    print(f"\nT1 = {T1}, T2 = {T2}:")
    print(f"  Valid (T2 <= 2*T1 = {constraint['T2_max']}): {constraint['valid']}")
    
    if constraint['valid']:
        if constraint['T_phi'] < np.inf:
            print(f"  Pure dephasing time T_phi = {constraint['T_phi']:.2f}")
        else:
            print(f"  T2 = 2*T1 (T1-limited, no pure dephasing)")
        
        rho_list = t1_t2_evolution(rho0, T1, T2, times)
        obs = extract_observables(rho_list)
        
        print(f"  At t=0: P(|1>)={obs['p1'][0]:.4f}, coh={obs['coherence'][0]:.4f}")
        print(f"  At t=T2: P(|1>)={obs['p1'][np.argmin(np.abs(times-T2))]:.4f}, "
              f"coh={obs['coherence'][np.argmin(np.abs(times-T2))]:.4f}")
        print(f"  At t=3*T1: P(|1>)={obs['p1'][-1]:.4f}, coh={obs['coherence'][-1]:.4f}")
    else:
        print(f"  INVALID: T2 > 2*T1 is physically impossible!")

# Compare T2-limited vs T1-limited regimes
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Comparing decoherence regimes:")

configs = [
    ("T2-limited (strong pure dephasing)", 100, 30),
    ("Balanced", 100, 80),
    ("T1-limited (T2 ≈ 2*T1)", 100, 195),
]

for name, T1, T2 in configs:
    constraint = verify_t2_constraint(T1, T2)
    rho_list = t1_t2_evolution(rho0, T1, T2, times)
    obs = extract_observables(rho_list)
    
    # Find time to lose half coherence
    half_coh_idx = np.argmin(np.abs(obs['coherence'] - 0.5 * obs['coherence'][0]))
    
    print(f"\n{name}:")
    print(f"  T1={T1}, T2={T2}, T_phi={constraint['T_phi']:.1f}")
    print(f"  Time to half coherence: {times[half_coh_idx]:.1f}")
    print(f"  Coherence at t=T1: {obs['coherence'][np.argmin(np.abs(times-T1))]:.4f}")

exercises.title: Channel Composition
exercises.description: Compose multiple decoherence channels and analyze the resulting effective channel.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def compose_channels(kraus1: list, kraus2: list) -> list:
    """
    Compose two channels: E2(E1(rho)).
    The composed Kraus operators are {E2_j E1_i} for all i,j.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def channel_to_choi(kraus_ops: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Convert Kraus operators to Choi matrix representation.
    Choi(E) = sum_ij E(|i><j|) tensor |i><j|
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def channel_fidelity(kraus1: list, kraus2: list) -> float:
    """
    Calculate fidelity between two channels using Choi matrices.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test: Compose amplitude and phase damping
gamma_ad = 0.3
gamma_pd = 0.2

kraus_ad = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma_ad)
kraus_pd = phase_damping_kraus(gamma_pd)

# Compose in both orders
kraus_ad_then_pd = compose_channels(kraus_ad, kraus_pd)
kraus_pd_then_ad = compose_channels(kraus_pd, kraus_ad)

print("Channel composition analysis:")
print(f"Amp damp (gamma={gamma_ad}) + Phase damp (gamma={gamma_pd})")
print(f"Number of composed Kraus ops: {len(kraus_ad_then_pd)}")

# Apply to |+> and compare
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_plus = ket_plus @ ket_plus.conj().T

rho_ad_pd = apply_channel(rho_plus, kraus_ad_then_pd)
rho_pd_ad = apply_channel(rho_plus, kraus_pd_then_ad)

print(f"\n|+> after AD then PD: coherence = {np.abs(rho_ad_pd[0,1]):.4f}")
print(f"|+> after PD then AD: coherence = {np.abs(rho_pd_ad[0,1]):.4f}")
print(f"Same result? {np.allclose(rho_ad_pd, rho_pd_ad)}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    E0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    E1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    return [E0, E1]

def phase_damping_kraus(lambda_p):
    E0 = np.sqrt(1-lambda_p) * np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
    E1 = np.sqrt(lambda_p) * np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    E2 = np.sqrt(lambda_p) * np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
    return [E0, E1, E2]

def depolarizing_kraus(p):
    sigma_x = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
    sigma_y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]], dtype=complex)
    sigma_z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)
    I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)
    return [np.sqrt(1-3*p/4)*I, np.sqrt(p/4)*sigma_x, 
            np.sqrt(p/4)*sigma_y, np.sqrt(p/4)*sigma_z]

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    result = np.zeros_like(rho)
    for E in kraus_ops:
        result += E @ rho @ E.conj().T
    return result

def compose_channels(kraus1: list, kraus2: list) -> list:
    """
    Compose two channels: E2(E1(rho)).
    """
    composed = []
    for E1 in kraus1:
        for E2 in kraus2:
            composed.append(E2 @ E1)
    return composed

def channel_to_choi(kraus_ops: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Convert Kraus operators to Choi matrix.
    """
    dim = kraus_ops[0].shape[0]
    choi = np.zeros((dim**2, dim**2), dtype=complex)
    
    for i in range(dim):
        for j in range(dim):
            # |i><j| basis element
            ij = np.zeros((dim, dim), dtype=complex)
            ij[i, j] = 1
            
            # Apply channel
            E_ij = apply_channel(ij, kraus_ops)
            
            # Choi: E(|i><j|) tensor |i><j|
            choi += np.kron(E_ij, ij)
    
    return choi

def channel_fidelity(kraus1: list, kraus2: list) -> float:
    """
    Calculate fidelity between two channels.
    F = Tr(sqrt(sqrt(C1) C2 sqrt(C1)))^2 / (Tr(C1) Tr(C2))
    Simplified for normalized Choi matrices.
    """
    from scipy.linalg import sqrtm
    
    C1 = channel_to_choi(kraus1)
    C2 = channel_to_choi(kraus2)
    
    # Normalize
    dim = kraus1[0].shape[0]
    C1 = C1 / dim
    C2 = C2 / dim
    
    # Fidelity
    sqrt_C1 = sqrtm(C1)
    inner = sqrtm(sqrt_C1 @ C2 @ sqrt_C1)
    return np.real(np.trace(inner))**2

# Test: Compose amplitude and phase damping
gamma_ad = 0.3
gamma_pd = 0.2

kraus_ad = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma_ad)
kraus_pd = phase_damping_kraus(gamma_pd)

# Compose in both orders
kraus_ad_then_pd = compose_channels(kraus_ad, kraus_pd)
kraus_pd_then_ad = compose_channels(kraus_pd, kraus_ad)

print("Channel Composition Analysis")
print("="*60)
print(f"Channel 1: Amplitude damping (gamma={gamma_ad})")
print(f"Channel 2: Phase damping (gamma={gamma_pd})")
print(f"\nNumber of Kraus ops: AD={len(kraus_ad)}, PD={len(kraus_pd)}")
print(f"Composed: {len(kraus_ad_then_pd)}")

# Verify completeness
def verify_complete(kraus):
    total = sum(E.conj().T @ E for E in kraus)
    return np.allclose(total, np.eye(2))

print(f"\nCompleteness check:")
print(f"  AD then PD: {verify_complete(kraus_ad_then_pd)}")
print(f"  PD then AD: {verify_complete(kraus_pd_then_ad)}")

# Apply to test states
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_plus = ket_plus @ ket_plus.conj().T
rho1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)

print(f"\nEffect on |+> state:")
rho_ad_pd = apply_channel(rho_plus, kraus_ad_then_pd)
rho_pd_ad = apply_channel(rho_plus, kraus_pd_then_ad)
print(f"  AD then PD: coherence = {np.abs(rho_ad_pd[0,1]):.4f}, P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_ad_pd[1,1]):.4f}")
print(f"  PD then AD: coherence = {np.abs(rho_pd_ad[0,1]):.4f}, P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_pd_ad[1,1]):.4f}")
print(f"  Order matters? {not np.allclose(rho_ad_pd, rho_pd_ad)}")

print(f"\nEffect on |1> state:")
rho_ad_pd_1 = apply_channel(rho1, kraus_ad_then_pd)
rho_pd_ad_1 = apply_channel(rho1, kraus_pd_then_ad)
print(f"  AD then PD: P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_ad_pd_1[1,1]):.4f}")
print(f"  PD then AD: P(|1>) = {np.real(rho_pd_ad_1[1,1]):.4f}")
print(f"  Same (no coherence to dephase): {np.allclose(rho_ad_pd_1, rho_pd_ad_1)}")

# Channel fidelity
print(f"\nChannel fidelities:")
fid = channel_fidelity(kraus_ad_then_pd, kraus_pd_then_ad)
print(f"  F(AD->PD, PD->AD) = {fid:.4f}")

# Repeated application
print(f"\nRepeated amplitude damping:")
rho = rho_plus.copy()
for n in range(5):
    coh = np.abs(rho[0, 1])
    p1 = np.real(rho[1, 1])
    print(f"  After {n} applications: coherence={coh:.4f}, P(|1>)={p1:.4f}")
    rho = apply_channel(rho, kraus_ad)
hints: Composed Kraus: {E2_j @ E1_i} for all i, j
Choi matrix: C = sum_ij E(|i><j|) tensor |i><j|
Order of composition matters for non-commuting channels

testCases.expected: Different from PD then AD (order matters)

exercises.title: Generalized Amplitude Damping
exercises.description: Implement generalized amplitude damping for finite-temperature thermalization.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def thermal_population(omega: float, T: float) -> float:
    """
    Calculate thermal ground state population.
    p = 1 / (1 + exp(-omega/T))
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def generalized_amplitude_damping(gamma: float, p: float) -> list:
    """
    Return Kraus operators for generalized amplitude damping.
    gamma: damping probability
    p: thermal ground state population
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def simulate_thermalization(rho0: np.ndarray, omega: float, T: float,
                            gamma_rate: float, times: np.ndarray) -> list:
    """
    Simulate thermalization using repeated application of GAD.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test thermalization
omega = 1.0  # Qubit frequency
T_values = [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0]  # Various temperatures

# Start from excited state
rho0 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)

print("Thermalization to different temperature baths:")
for T in T_values:
    p_thermal = thermal_population(omega, T)
    p1_eq = 1 - p_thermal  # Equilibrium excited population
    
    times = np.linspace(0, 10, 50)
    rho_list = simulate_thermalization(rho0, omega, T, 0.5, times)
    
    p1_final = np.real(rho_list[-1][1, 1])
    print(f"  T={T:.1f}: p1_eq={p1_eq:.4f}, p1_final={p1_final:.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def thermal_population(omega: float, T: float) -> float:
    """
    Calculate thermal ground state population.
    """
    if T < 1e-10:
        return 1.0  # Zero temperature -> ground state
    return 1 / (1 + np.exp(-omega / T))

def generalized_amplitude_damping(gamma: float, p: float) -> list:
    """
    Return Kraus operators for generalized amplitude damping.
    """
    E0 = np.sqrt(p) * np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]], dtype=complex)
    E1 = np.sqrt(p) * np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]], dtype=complex)
    E2 = np.sqrt(1-p) * np.array([[np.sqrt(1-gamma), 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
    E3 = np.sqrt(1-p) * np.array([[0, 0], [np.sqrt(gamma), 0]], dtype=complex)
    return [E0, E1, E2, E3]

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    result = np.zeros_like(rho)
    for E in kraus_ops:
        result += E @ rho @ E.conj().T
    return result

def simulate_thermalization(rho0: np.ndarray, omega: float, T: float,
                            gamma_rate: float, times: np.ndarray) -> list:
    """
    Simulate thermalization using GAD.
    """
    p_thermal = thermal_population(omega, T)
    results = [rho0.copy()]
    
    dt = times[1] - times[0] if len(times) > 1 else 1.0
    gamma_step = 1 - np.exp(-gamma_rate * dt)
    
    kraus = generalized_amplitude_damping(gamma_step, p_thermal)
    
    rho = rho0.copy()
    for _ in times[1:]:
        rho = apply_channel(rho, kraus)
        results.append(rho.copy())
    
    return results

def theoretical_thermal_state(omega: float, T: float) -> np.ndarray:
    """Return the theoretical thermal equilibrium state."""
    p0 = thermal_population(omega, T)
    p1 = 1 - p0
    return np.diag([p0, p1]).astype(complex)

# Test thermalization
omega = 1.0
T_values = [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0]

print("Thermalization Analysis")
print("="*60)

# Test from both initial states
initial_states = {
    '|0>': np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]], dtype=complex),
    '|1>': np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex),
    '|+>': np.array([[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]], dtype=complex)
}

for T in T_values:
    p_thermal = thermal_population(omega, T)
    p1_eq = 1 - p_thermal
    rho_thermal = theoretical_thermal_state(omega, T)
    
    print(f"\nT = {T:.1f} (equilibrium P(|1>) = {p1_eq:.4f}):")
    
    times = np.linspace(0, 20, 100)
    
    for name, rho0 in initial_states.items():
        rho_list = simulate_thermalization(rho0, omega, T, 0.5, times)
        rho_final = rho_list[-1]
        
        p1_final = np.real(rho_final[1, 1])
        distance = np.linalg.norm(rho_final - rho_thermal)
        
        print(f"  {name}: P(|1>)_final = {p1_final:.4f}, ||rho - rho_th|| = {distance:.4f}")

# Detailed evolution for one case
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Detailed evolution from |1> at T=1.0:")
T = 1.0
rho0 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
times = np.linspace(0, 20, 50)
rho_list = simulate_thermalization(rho0, omega, T, 0.5, times)

p_thermal = thermal_population(omega, T)
print(f"Equilibrium P(|1>) = {1-p_thermal:.4f}")
print("\nTime evolution:")
for i in [0, 10, 20, 30, 49]:
    rho = rho_list[i]
    p1 = np.real(rho[1, 1])
    purity = np.real(np.trace(rho @ rho))
    print(f"  t={times[i]:.1f}: P(|1>)={p1:.4f}, purity={purity:.4f}")

# Temperature dependence of steady state
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Temperature dependence of equilibrium:")
print(f"{'T':<8} {'P(|0>)':<10} {'P(|1>)':<10} {'Purity':<10}")
for T in [0.01, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0]:
    rho_th = theoretical_thermal_state(omega, T)
    purity = np.real(np.trace(rho_th @ rho_th))
    print(f"{T:<8.2f} {np.real(rho_th[0,0]):<10.4f} {np.real(rho_th[1,1]):<10.4f} {purity:<10.4f}")
hints: GAD has 4 Kraus operators for emission and absorption
At T->0, reduces to standard amplitude damping

testCases.expected: Equilibrates to thermal state with correct populations
